mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects broad economic strength, leading to higher aggregate demand and corporate earnings expectations. This positive signal drives equity prices higher over the subsequent month.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS is a coincident indicator of economic activity.
failure_condition: The signal's walk-forward information coefficient (rank IC) for 20-day returns remains below 0.02 for a continuous 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When the effective federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) rises above a 2% threshold, it signals a transition to a restrictive monetary policy stance. This increases the discount rate for future corporate earnings and tightens financial conditions, negatively impacting equity valuations over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS value is 2.04, just above the proposed trigger.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when the trigger condition is met over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: An interaction where strong retail sales (RSAFS) growth co-occurs with a sharp contraction in housing starts (HOUST) signals a late-cycle divergence. Resilient consumption masks underlying weakness in interest-rate sensitive sectors, creating a fragile environment prone to negative equity corrections as the broader economy decelerates.
failure_condition: The dual-proxy signal, when triggered, is not followed by negative S&P 500 returns within 60 days for a majority of occurrences in a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: In a low unemployment regime (UNRATE below 4%), a modest increase in the 3-month moving average of the unemployment rate is a powerful leading indicator of recession (the 'Sahm Rule' principle). This signals a cyclical peak and triggers a risk-off rotation out of equities.
candidate_trigger: 3-month avg of UNRATE rises > 0.3 from its 12-month low
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE of 3.5% places the economy in the specified low-unemployment regime.
failure_condition: When the trigger condition is met within the specified regime, the S&P 500 does not experience a drawdown of at least 5% over the subsequent 60-day period in at least 2 out of 3 consecutive instances.

mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates a healthy and expanding supply side of the economy. This translates to robust corporate revenue growth and improved investor sentiment, leading to positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: A strategy going long the S&P 500 when the 3-month change in INDPRO is positive fails to outperform a buy-and-hold strategy over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) significantly exceeds the policy rate (FEDFUNDS), it indicates the central bank is 'behind the curve'. This interaction creates expectations of future aggressive monetary tightening, increasing market volatility and causing a repricing of equity risk premia downwards.
failure_condition: The Sharpe ratio of the S&P 500 over the 60 days following a trigger event is not statistically lower (p > 0.10) than the Sharpe ratio during non-triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in real GDP growth (GDPC1), even while positive, signals a loss of economic momentum. This 'growth scare' leads to downward revisions in earnings forecasts and causes investors to demand a higher equity risk premium, resulting in negative market performance.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth falls below 1.0%
failure_condition: In periods following a trigger, S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive more than 50% of the time over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: A surprisingly large month-over-month drop in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) is interpreted by markets as an immediate positive signal of economic resilience, boosting sentiment and driving positive equity returns over a very short horizon before longer-term inflation concerns dominate.
failure_condition: The average 5-day S&P 500 return following a trigger event is not statistically different from zero (t-test p > 0.05) over a 10-year sample.
